subsection.heading: Authority.—
subsection.chapeau: The Administrator may pay, on a case-by-case basis, a bonus under this section to an employee of the Agency if—

paragraph.content: the unusually high or unique qualifications of the employee or a special need of the Agency for the employee’s services makes it essential to retain the employee; and

paragraph.chapeau: the Administrator determines that, in the absence of such a bonus, the employee would be likely to leave—

subparagraph.content: for a different position in the Federal service.

subsection.heading: Service Agreement.—
subsection.chapeau: Payment of a bonus under this section is contingent upon the employee entering into a written service agreement with the Agency to complete a period of service with the Agency. Such agreement shall include—

paragraph.content: the period of service the individual shall be required to complete in return for the bonus; and
the conditions under which the agreement may be terminated before the agreed-upon service period has been completed, and the effect of the termination.

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: The amount of a bonus under this section shall be determined by the Administrator, but may not exceed 25 percent of the annual rate of basic pay of the position involved.

paragraph.heading: Form of payment.—
paragraph.content: A bonus under this section shall be paid in the form of a lump-sum payment and shall not be considered to be part of basic pay.
may not be based on any period of service which is the basis for a recruitment bonus under section 10104;

paragraph.chapeau: may not be paid to an individual who is appointed to or holds—

subparagraph.content: a position to which an individual is appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate;
a position in the Senior Executive Service as a noncareer appointee (as defined in section 3132(a)); or
a position which has been excepted from the competitive service by reason of its confidential, policy-determining, policy-making, or policy-advocating character; and

paragraph.content: upon completion of the strategic human capital plan, shall be paid in accordance with that plan.

subsection.heading: Termination of Authority.—
subsection.content: The authority to grant bonuses under this section shall expire 5 years after the date of enactment of this chapter.

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: The Office of Personnel Management shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress, annually for each of the first 5 years during which this section is in effect, a report on the operation of this section.

paragraph.heading: Contents.—
paragraph.chapeau: Each report submitted under this subsection shall include, with respect to the period covered by such report, a description of how the authority to pay bonuses under this section was used by the Agency, including, with respect to each such agency—

subparagraph.content: the number and dollar amount of bonuses paid to individuals holding positions within each pay grade, pay level, or other pay classification; and
a determination of the extent to which such bonuses furthered the purposes of this section.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The date of enactment of this chapter, referred to in subsec. (e), is the date of enactment of Pub. L. 109–295, which was approved Oct. 4, 2006.
